title: Syrupy Middle Eastern King Prawns Tagine
description: A resinous, gooey tagine that caramelizes king prawns with honey and date syrup for an adventurous Middle Eastern flavor.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a heavy-bottomed pot over medium heat.
Add chopped onion, garlic, and ginger, sauté until the onion turns translucent and begins to caramelize.
Stir in cumin, coriander, smoked paprika, cinnamon, and a pinch of saffron, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add canned tomatoes, honey, date syrup, lemon juice, water, salt, pepper, and bay leaf; bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Place the king prawns into the sauce, cover, and simmer for 3 to 4 minutes until the prawns are pink and opaque.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let the sauce thicken for an additional 3 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Taste and adjust seasoning, adding more salt, pepper, or honey if desired for extra sweetness.
Serve the tagine hot over steaming couscous or flatbread, garnishing with chopped fresh cilantro if desired.
